items.title: Akira Ikezoe’s Frogs and Bears Have Something Urgent to Tell Us
items.summary: The cartoonish earnestness of both artist and artwork belies a sharp attentiveness to the catastrophes unfolding around us.

items.title: Charles Seliger Painted Nature’s Invisible Architecture
items.summary: One of the youngest Abstract Expressionists, the artist charted an independent path guided by the beauty and intricacy of cellular structures.

items.title: A View From the Easel With Arghavan Khosravi
items.summary: “It feels like I am spending my time doing what I love.”

items.title: Revisiting 35 Years of an Iconic Newark Artist-Led Space
items.summary: Aljira championed the work of Dawoud Bey, Firelei Báez, Jeffrey Gibson, and other socially engaged artists who critiqued gentrification and capitalism.
